title: Smart-pointer aliases overlapped flipped constructor parameters
summary: Generic $up/$sp aliases and whole-parameter movement both decorated std::unique_ptr and std::shared_ptr constructor parameters, producing broken mixed spellings instead of the compact ownership type.
details: The reported std::unique_ptr<Linalg> linalg parameter displayed as a mixture containing the legacy $up shorthand rather than linalg: Linalg^.
aliases.lua owned the entire flipped parameter range while its generic alias table also placed a second extmark inside that same range.
The previous flat strip_type logic only recognized top-level unique/shared pointers, omitted weak_ptr, and did not give raw type positions the same recursive behavior.
The correction removes generic source smart-pointer aliases, preserves authoring snippets separately, and routes every type consumer through one balanced recursive semantic grammar.
Parameter ownership remains with aliases.lua; pointer.lua handles outermost raw templates only when no enclosing parameter owner exists, and dependent module toggles repaint both owners.
A headless Visual harness gap was also corrected by delivering the endpoint CursorMoved and exit lifecycle events explicitly, ensuring every selected row is validated.

provenance.note: Owner reported incorrect unique_ptr handling in a multiline constructor and requested symmetric refinement with weak_ptr and optional.

provenance.reference: former aliases.lua ALIASES smart-pointer entries and flat parse.lua strip_type branches
provenance.note: Static audit and generated corpora isolated overlapping ownership plus incomplete grammar coverage.

evidence.claim: The exact constructor, smart/optional pairs, 125-row generated renderer corpus, colors, Visual reveal, and aliases toggle cycles are regression-tested.
Existing golden and parameter layout coverage now expects semantic ownership spelling rather than the broken legacy alias.

questions: Why did std::unique_ptr<Linalg> render with $up in a constructor?
How are smart pointers kept symmetric across parameters and returns?
Why does toggling aliases also refresh pointer rendering?

last_verified.method: Reported constructor reproduced, root causes isolated with generated corpora, and the shared grammar verified through focused and full-suite interaction coverage.
